test.observation: 1139 tests passed, 0 failed, 9 ignored. Up from 499 in round 1 — new provider tests added.

lint.observation: Zero clippy warnings. Was 30 pre-existing errors in round 1; all have been fixed.

nonBlockingIssues.description: disconnect() sends ProviderCommand::Shutdown then immediately aborts the worker handle. The Shutdown command never executes — abort() preempts it. The abort path is valid cleanup, making the Shutdown send dead code in the disconnect flow.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Remove the Shutdown send from disconnect() and rely solely on abort(), or remove abort() and wait for graceful Shutdown processing.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Tests exercise MockProvider rather than CodexProvider directly due to AppServerClient being difficult to mock. CodexProvider's specific disconnect logic (Shutdown + abort) is not directly tested.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Consider adding at least one integration test that exercises the real CodexProvider disconnect path if a real AppServerClient becomes available in test.

nonBlockingIssues.description: CodexProvider::new() sets connected=true immediately; connect() returns ConnectionFailed when not connected. Once disconnected, CodexProvider cannot be reconnected — a new instance must be created. This design limitation is documented in code comments but could be more prominent.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add a doc comment on ProviderTransport::connect() explaining the no-reconnect contract for CodexProvider.

nonBlockingIssues.description: FileChangeDelta maps to UiEvent::CommandOutputDelta, meaning the UI cannot distinguish file changes from command output at the UiEvent level. Both render as streaming text.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Acceptable for now. Consider a dedicated UiEvent::FileChangeDelta variant in future if typed file-change rendering is needed.

nonBlockingIssues.description: ToolCallStarted/ToolCallUpdate params include the serde type discriminator tag because they use serde_json::to_value(event). Downstream consumers may not expect the 'type' field in params.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Consider stripping the type field or constructing params manually from variant fields.

nonBlockingIssues.description: 11 of 28 secondary notification variants lack dedicated runtime test coverage for their Unknown mapping. Compile-time exhaustiveness is guaranteed (no wildcard arms), but method name/payload verification is missing for those variants.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add runtime test coverage for remaining secondary variants when convenient.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The 'replace active session during connect' path is tested via direct method call but not end-to-end, because existing_active_session causes reuse instead of replacement.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add an end-to-end test that forces replacement by making the first session appear disconnected while keeping it in the map.

nonBlockingIssues.description: ProviderConfig construction test is structural (constructs config directly) rather than exercising the actual production code path inside connect_remote_over_ssh_with_agent_type.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Consider using a mock to intercept the config passed to create_provider_over_ssh for more robust verification.

nonBlockingIssues.description: regression_broadcast_lagged_handled_gracefully tests raw tokio broadcast channel rather than provider layer. Other two lagged tests do cover the provider path, so overall VAL-ABS-039 coverage is adequate.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Low priority — coverage is adequate via the other two lagged tests.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Thread-local storage (SENT_REQUESTS, SENT_NOTIFICATIONS) in mock providers relies on single-threaded test runtime default. Would break if test configuration changes to multi-threaded runtime.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Consider using Arc<Mutex<Vec<>>> instead of thread_local! for thread-safe tracking.

nonBlockingIssues.description: AGENTS.md says 'make rust-test runs all 1037+ tests' but the actual count is now 1139. The test count threshold in AGENTS.md is stale.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Update AGENTS.md to reflect current test count.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update test count from '1037+' to '1139+' to reflect current actual count after provider abstraction milestone.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: provider-milestone-build-gate review notes the count discrepancy.

rejectedObservations.observation: ProviderConfig is internal-only (no uniffi, no serde) — should be documented in AGENTS.md.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in .factory/library/provider-transport.md. Adding to AGENTS.md would duplicate and AGENTS.md is for normative guidance, not implementation details.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature description in features.json mentions wrong AgentType variants (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Custom, Factory instead of actual Codex, PiAcp, PiNative, DroidAcp, DroidNative, GenericAcp).
rejectedObservations.reason: Features.json is mission-level planning metadata, not code. The validation contract correctly specifies the actual variants. Not actionable for code quality.

rejectedObservations.observation: The $resolve/$reject magic method names in from_provider are not documented in library/.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already noted in round 1 synthesis as a non-blocking issue. Provider-transport.md adequately describes the event mapping. Adding magic method documentation is low priority since it's an internal implementation detail.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate mock implementations (TestProvider in mobile_client/tests.rs and TestMockProvider in session/connection.rs) are not documented.
rejectedObservations.reason: Test mock duplication is normal for different testing scopes. Not guidance-worthy.

rejectedObservations.observation: Pre-existing broken iOS test methods (ConversationStreamingViewportPolicyTests) suggest iOS tests may not be regularly executed.
rejectedObservations.reason: Valid observation but not actionable by the scrutiny validator. The build-gate worker already fixed the broken tests. Not a code quality issue for this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Pre-existing LitterUITests.testCaptureScreenshots failure is a UI test infrastructure issue.
rejectedObservations.reason: Known infrastructure issue unrelated to the provider milestone. Not actionable here.
